Egypt.status: Leading the group, a draw today likely secures top spot outright

Iran.status: Needs a win today to keep real control of 2nd place, since Belgium is a heavy favorite in the other GD3 fixture

matches._note: Iran's offside count (2, then 4) held up or RISE even as their possession collapsed from 48.5% to 29.9% — this is a counter-attacking, transition-based team, not a settle-and-defend team. Do not apply the defensive-underdog 2+ offsides NO-family here without checking this.

Iran_plus0_5_corners.note: derived: Egypt+0.5 covers (ties+Egypt more)=0.5983, so P(Iran>Egypt)=0.4017

smarkets_markets.Mahmoud_Trezeguet: NOT LISTED in any player market for this match (SOT, anytime goalscorer, or score-or-assist) — same structural absence pattern as McGree (USA-AUS) and Berterame (MEX-KOR), both of whom were confirmed not in the matchday squad.

context_flags: TREZEGUET_ABSENT_FLAG: Trezeguet — Egypt's hero from the NZL-EGY match (goal+assist, our biggest single-player win of the tournament at +32.04) — is absent from every Smarkets player market for this match. RULE9 pattern (McGree/Berterame): this has been 100% predictive of a confirmed squad absence in 2 prior instances. The crowd, anchored on his heroic last performance plus general 'winner narrative' bias, will likely overprice him; we should price near-zero.
EVEN_MATCH_BUT_DRAW_HEAVY: FTR is close to a 3-way pick'em (EGY 36.1% / IRN 28.6% / Draw 36.0%, the highest draw price of any GD3 fixture analyzed) — RULE8 cagey-match signal is strong here.

IRN_COUNTERATTACK_PROFILE: Iran's offside count actually rose as their possession fell (2 at 48.5% poss, 4 at 29.9% poss) — they are a transition/counter team that generates offside risk independent of possession share, unlike a typical low-block defensive underdog. Trust their raw offside rate rather than discounting it for underdog status.
EGY_STAKES_LOWER: Egypt already control the group (4pts) and a draw is likely enough for top spot — some chance of a slightly more conservative approach than their NZL demolition.

questions.text: Will Iran be caught offside 2 or more times?
questions.reasoning: Iran offsides: 2 (vs NZL, 48.5% poss) and 4 (vs Belgium, only 29.9% poss). λ=3.0 → raw Poisson P(≥2)=0.80. This is the rare underdog case (see IRN_COUNTERATTACK_PROFILE) where the usual 2+ offsides NO-family does NOT apply — Iran's offside rate is driven by transition attacking, not possession share, and it held up (rose, even) against a much stronger opponent. Trust the data over the generic underdog discount.

questions.text: Will Iran have more shots on target than Egypt in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Half-split Skellam off full-match ESPN averages (IRN λ_2H=1.75, EGY λ_2H=2.5) gives P(IRN>EGY)=0.27.

questions.text: Will Iran finish with more corner kicks than Egypt?
questions.reasoning: Derived from the Egypt+0.5/Iran-0.5 corners handicap market: P(Iran strictly more) = 1 - 0.5983 = 0.40.
Smarkets FTR: Egypt 36.10%. Direct tier-1 market in a near-3-way-even, draw-heavy fixture.

questions.text: Will the match have 2 or fewer total goals?
questions.reasoning: Direct market: Under2.5 = 65.39%. Consistent with the high draw probability and generally low-event profile both teams have shown.

questions.text: Will the second half have 2 or more total goals?
Will there be 4 or more total cards shown?
questions.reasoning: ESPN combined average (EGY 1.5 + IRN 1.0 = 2.5 total) gives Poisson P(≥4)=0.24; the Over2.5-cards market (53.4%, implying total λ≈2.82) gives P(≥4)=0.31. Blend the two.

questions.text: Will Egypt have 3 or more shots on target?
questions.reasoning: ESPN-history Poisson (λ=5.0) gives P(≥3)=0.88; the market-implied rate from the Over3.5 SOT line (54.1%, λ≈3.87) gives P(≥3)=0.74. Blend toward the middle, weighting the matchup-specific market somewhat since Iran is a tougher defensive test than New Zealand.

questions.text: Will Mahmoud Trezeguet have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.reasoning: Trezeguet does not appear in ANY player market for this match (SOT, goalscorer, or score-or-assist) — identical structural pattern to McGree (USA-AUS, confirmed absent, won us +20.74) and Berterame (MEX-KOR, confirmed absent). The crowd will likely anchor on his heroic NZL-EGY performance and price him generously; we price near-zero on the absence signal.

questions.text: Will Mehdi Taremi have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.reasoning: Illiquid full-match offer (0.6135, adjusted ×0.945=0.580). Taremi is Iran's primary striker (Inter Milan); Iran is only a moderate underdog here (28.6%, not extreme), so no RULE15 suppression applies. Trust the market near-raw.

strongest_picks: Q9: Trezeguet SOT = 0.10 (MAXIMUM CONFIDENCE — absent from every player market, same pattern as 2 prior confirmed-absent players, crowd likely to overprice given his last match heroics)
Q1: Iran 2+ offsides = 0.75 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — genuine counter-attacking signal that held up even as possession collapsed, NOT a defensive-underdog situation)
Q8: Egypt 3+ SOT = 0.80 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — blended ESPN + market both point strongly YES)
